title: Thinking layer (full) — add recall-merge + clarifier + memory-id extraction
depends_on: T-124 (lite version must ship and prove out first)
current_state: T-124-lite ships with two jobs (intent + normalised_query). Three additional jobs were deferred: memory-id extraction from recall hits, explicit clarifier-question generation, confidence calibration. These add quality but compound prompt complexity; ship them only after lite proves stable.
structural_problem: Five jobs in one LLM call produces inconsistent output. Phased rollout proves each piece independently.
target_state: agent/thinking.py extended: normalise() output gains referenced_memories: [L3 ids] field (from recall hits) and ask_clarifier: str|null field (generated when confidence<0.6). Prompt grows to handle the merged context. T-124-lite must have ≥7 days of clean operation before T-129 ships.
design_notes: Only ship after T-124-lite shows green in /passive for 7 consecutive days
Prompt extension: '...also given recall hits <list>, identify any of the user's references to past facts (cite ids)'
Clarifier generation: replaces the lite version's static 'ask one clarifying question' with model-generated question
Maintain backward compat: if extended call fails, fall back to lite output shape
success_criteria: T-124-lite has 7+ days of clean operation in /passive
Memory-id extraction recovers L3 ids from recall hits with >80% accuracy on test corpus
Clarifier questions are concrete (not 'can you clarify')
risk_notes: Prompt creep risk; could regress lite-mode quality. Mitigation: A/B compare; abort if lite quality drops.
